evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

Image source, Green Party Image caption, Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Rejoin EU Peter Ward has worked as a barrister in Manchester The Rejoin EU party have announced that Peter Ward will be their candidate for next month's by-election in Makerfield The party said "rejoining the EU has come back into the British political debate in a big way" and that it wanted to "make a full contribution to this discussion" Mr Ward, who has worked as a barrister in Manchester, will stand in the Makerfield contest on Thursday 18 June The by-election follows the recent resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

similar_previous.semantic_text: Makerfield by-election candidates announced Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

similar_previous.semantic_text: Conservatives name Michael Winstanley for Makerfield by-election Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Rejoin EU Peter Ward has worked as a barrister in Manchester The Rejoin EU party have announced that Peter Ward will be their candidate for next month's by-election in Makerfield The party said "rejoining the EU has come back into the British political debate in a big way" and that it wanted to "make a full contribution to this discussion" Mr Ward, who has worked as a barrister in Manchester, will stand in the Makerfield contest on Thursday 18 June The by-election follows the recent resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

similar_previous.semantic_text: Makerfield by-election candidates announced Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

similar_previous.semantic_text: Conservatives name Michael Winstanley for Makerfield by-election Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

similar_previous.semantic_text: Makerfield by-election candidates announced Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

similar_previous.semantic_text: Conservatives name Michael Winstanley for Makerfield by-election Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Rejoin EU Peter Ward has worked as a barrister in Manchester The Rejoin EU party have announced that Peter Ward will be their candidate for next month's by-election in Makerfield The party said "rejoining the EU has come back into the British political debate in a big way" and that it wanted to "make a full contribution to this discussion" Mr Ward, who has worked as a barrister in Manchester, will stand in the Makerfield contest on Thursday 18 June The by-election follows the recent resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

similar_previous.normalized_title: makerfield by election candidates announced
similar_previous.semantic_text: Makerfield by-election candidates announced Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Who is standing for Parliament in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield? Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

similar_previous.semantic_text: Conservatives name Michael Winstanley for Makerfield by-election Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Former Wigan Mayor Michael Winstanley is named as the Conservative Party's candidate Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Angela Ferguson North West The Conservative Party Former Wigan mayor Michael Winstanley has been selected to stand for The Conservative Party in the upcoming by-election The Conservative Party has named its candidate for next month's Makerfie Former Mayor of Wigan Michael Winstanley will represent the Tories in the by-election being held in the Greater Manchester constituency on Thursday 18 June The poll was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons, who stepped down to allow Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham an opportunity to contest the seat and seek a return to Westminster Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch said Winstanley would "make an excellent champion of the area having lived in, worked in and represented the local community for years" Four other candidates have so far been announced as standing in the Makerfiel

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

evidence_packet.lead: Manchester has never stood still.
evidence_packet.summary: Manchester has never stood still. It is a city in constant motion
evidence_packet.evidence_text: Manchester has never stood still. It is a city in constant motion. A place that has looked to shape and adapt to economic change. The strapline we’re carrying into MIPIM this week says it all: Manchester: Breaking Boundaries, Building Tomorrow Alongside this strapline we will be using a refreshed Invest Manchester identity across all our activity. We have made this change to reflect the city region’s ambitions and to bring together MIDAS and Marketing Manchester’s promotion activity. We want the world to be as excited Invest Manchester will be the single market facing identity for all our business investment work. Whether that is real estate, foreign direct investment, sector and place development, or international partnerships. Crucially, it will provide us with a collaborative platform, alon The investmanchester.co.uk website is live. We will work together with partners as we build content over the next six months, roll out our brand identity, and bring forward new campaigns We’re at a pivotal moment for the city region. Greater Manchester has built something remarkable. Average annual economic growth of 3.1% for the last ten years, over double the national average; 100,000 new homes delivered; an integrated public transport system; and population gr Over the last decade, we’ve attracted more foreign direct investment than any other UK regional city. We have a city centre that’s home to global brands such as BNY, Microsoft, Google, AWS, Puma, S&P Global; SoHo House has launched, we’ve hosted the Brit Awards and the Chanel run
